title: Rakuten fixes issues twice as fast with Codex
optimized_headline: Rakuten Halves Issue Resolution Time Using Innovative Codex Technology
raw_excerpt: Rakuten uses Codex, the coding agent from OpenAI, to ship software faster and safer, reducing MTTR 50%, automating CI/CD reviews, and delivering full-stack builds in weeks.
raw_body: Rakuten uses Codex, the coding agent from OpenAI, to ship software faster and safer, reducing MTTR 50%, automating CI/CD reviews, and delivering full-stack builds in weeks.
speedrun: Rakuten has significantly improved its software development process by implementing Codex, OpenAI's coding agent. They've reduced their Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR) by 50%, which means they can fix issues much faster. Additionally, Codex automates continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) reviews, allowing for full-stack builds to be completed in just weeks. This enhancement is important as it enables Rakuten to deliver updates and fixes more efficiently, improving overall service quality.
why_it_matters: For developers and operations teams, this means quicker issue resolution, enhancing productivity and reducing downtime.
On a broader scale, this shift highlights the growing reliance on AI tools in software development, potentially reshaping industry standards.

lenses.eli12: Rakuten is using a smart coding assistant called Codex to help them write and fix software faster. Imagine if a chef had a helper that could chop vegetables and stir pots, allowing them to cook meals more quickly. This matters to everyone because faster software updates can lead to better apps and services we all use daily.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, Rakuten's use of Codex illustrates a strong user need for speed and reliability in software development. By cutting down recovery times and automating processes, companies could save on costs and improve efficiency. This could lead to faster product iterations and a better user experience.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, Rakuten's integration of Codex has led to a 50% reduction in MTTR, showcasing its effectiveness in automating CI/CD processes. This means that full-stack builds are now completed in weeks instead of longer timelines. Such improvements could set new benchmarks for development speeds in the industry.
